preamble.summary: Enoch travels eastward to a wilderness filled with trees and sees the fragrant plants that grow there, especially aromatic spices.
preamble.remarkable: The geographical tour eastward from Jerusalem toward paradise echoes the Eden narrative (Genesis 2:8, 'a garden in Eden, in the east').
preamble.connections: Genesis 2:8 (garden in the east); Song of Solomon 4:14 (fragrant plants); Ezekiel 47:12 (trees on the riverbanks).

verses.text_reference: And thence I went towards the east, into the midst of the mountain range of the desert, and I saw a wilderness and it was solitary, full of trees and plants.
verses.rendering: From there I went eastward, into the midst of a desert mountain range, and I saw a wilderness — solitary but full of trees and plants.

verses.text_reference: And water gushed forth from above.
Rushing like a copious watercourse [which flowed] towards the north-west it caused clouds and dew to ascend on every side.
verses.rendering: It rushed like a copious stream toward the north-west, causing clouds and dew to rise on every side.
translator_notes: The self-watering wilderness anticipates the eschatological transformation of deserts (Isaiah 35:1-2, 'the wilderness and the dry land shall be glad').
